text.tspan: Drawn by M. Minard, Inspector General of Bridges and Roads in retirement.
GRAPHIC TABLE of the temperature in degrees of the Réaumur thermometer below zero
of the successive losses in men of the French Army in the
The numbers of men present are represented by the widths of the coloured zones in a rate of one millimetre for ten thousand men; these are also written beside
the zones. Red designates men moving into Russia, black those on retreat. – The information used for drawing the map was taken
from the works of Messrs. Chiers, de Ségur, de Fezensac, de Chambray and the unpublished diary of Jacob, pharmacist of the Army since 28 October.
In order to aid the judgment of the eye regarding the diminution of the army, I supposed that the troops under Prince Jérôme and under Marshall Davoust, who were sent to Minsk
and Mobilow and who rejoined near Orsha and Vitebsk, had always marched with the army.
